title: LoRa coverage along a perimeter patrol road from a single ranger-camp gateway
summary: A ranger camp's existing LoRa gateway already covers near-field IoT (camera traps, fence sensors). The reserve now wants to instrument its patrol fleet with low-cost LoRa GPS trackers for routine after-action review and vehicle-down alerting. Op B from the gateway with the vehicle-mounted tracker as `rx_template` tells the operator which sections of the perimeter patrol road remain uplink-viable in real time, which sections require the tracker to buffer and store-and-forward on return-to-camp, and which sections will need a second gateway before any tracker deployment makes sense.
demonstrates: lora link-type plugin SF planning along a road corridor

sites.name: ranger-camp-malilangwe-gateway
sites.notes: Existing 18 m mast at Malilangwe main camp; mains+solar.

notes: The vehicle-tracker-lora-868 profile assumes a 2 m AGL roof whip — substitute vehicle-tracker-lte-catm1 to compare against cellular Cat-M1 coverage where a public LTE B20 cell is reachable. Operators wanting only the road-corridor view should clip the resulting raster to a buffered patrol-road geometry post-hoc; v2 does not support polyline-shaped AOIs in Op B (§4.0). The gateway-side equipment matches fence-line, rhino-collar, and camera-trap-mesh scenarios so an operator can colocate tracker uplinks on existing infrastructure without introducing new mast equipment.
